source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from ISO 3471 / OECD Code 4 two-post rear-mount ROPS geometry used by the ASABE Quarter-Scale Tractor competition: two vertical main roll-bar tube sections, transverse cross tubes tying the tops and feet together, welded gusset plates at each tube-to-tube and tube-to-baseplate intersection, two mounting base plates bolted to the chassis (the original 500 x 80 mm M12 x 4 pattern sits on these), and a seat-back restraint bar behind the operator SIP. The original prompt also calls out a drawbar pull at the rear of the chassis; the drawbar hitch is a separate part but is outside the ROPS itself. The assembly is a welded frame of 12-18 weldments, consistent with SAE J2194 ROPS geometry.

full_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic Roll-Over Protective Structure (ROPS) for a quarter-scale agricultural tractor. Gross vehicle weight (GVW) shall not exceed 900 lb (408 kg), the fixed powertrain is a small-displacement single-cylinder engine (approximately 25 hp), and minimum wheel track is 24 in (610 mm). The ROPS is a two-post rear-mount roll bar welded from 50.8 mm (2 in) OD x 3.2 mm (0.125 in) wall mild steel tubing (ASTM A513, E = 200 GPa, yield = 295 MPa, ultimate = 400 MPa, density = 7850 kg/m^3). Geometric constraints: ROPS height above operator seat reference point (SIP) >= 800 mm, ROPS width outside post-to-post 900 mm, post-to-seat clearance envelope >= 50 mm. Mounted to chassis via four M12 bolts on a 500 mm x 80 mm rectangular pattern. Load cases derived from ISO 3471 / OECD Code 4 scaled to GVW 408 kg: (R1) Lateral load: 1.75 x GVW x g = 1.75 x 408 x 9.81 = 7,000 N applied horizontally to the top of the ROPS; max von Mises in tube <= yield = 295 MPa; plastic deflection volume within Deflection Limiting Volume (DLV) bounds (operator seat envelope shall not be penetrated). (R2) Vertical crush: 2.0 x GVW x g = 8,000 N applied vertically to the top of the ROPS; max von Mises <= ultimate / 1.5 = 267 MPa. (R3) Longitudinal load: 0.5 x GVW x g = 2,000 N applied longitudinally at the top; max von Mises <= 295 MPa. (R4) Drawbar pull event: 1,500 N applied at the drawbar hitch point on the rear of the chassis while ROPS is loaded with operator (80 kg) weight; this is a coupling check at the ROPS-to-chassis bolt interface; bolt-hole bearing <= yield = 295 MPa. (R5) ROPS mass <= 12 kg. Primary verification: structural FEA, linear static, four independent load cases. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 welded frame, weld map, and a report listing mass in kg, max deflection under R1 in mm (must stay outside DLV), max von Mises per load case in MPa.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
The deliverable is reconfigured as the full welded ROPS assembly (12-18 weldments consistent with SAE J2194 / ISO 3471 rear-mount two-post geometry). Parts: two main roll-bar vertical tubes forming the two posts, transverse cross tubes tying the posts together at the top (and optionally at mid-height), welded gusset plates at each tube-to-tube and tube-to-baseplate intersection (typically 6-8), two mounting base plates bolted to the chassis via the 500 x 80 mm M12 pattern, and a seat-back restraint bar behind the operator SIP. FEM verification runs on the assembled ROPS only; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Weld-zone and bolt-pattern DCR must be <= 1.0 under each ISO 3471 load case. All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim.
short_prompt:
Design a Roll-Over Protective Structure (ROPS) for a quarter-scale agricultural tractor as a welded multi-part assembly. GVW <= 900 lb (408 kg); minimum wheel track 24 in (610 mm). Two-post rear-mount roll bar in 50.8 mm (2 in) OD x 3.2 mm (0.125 in) wall mild steel tubing (ASTM A513; E = 200 GPa, yield = 295 MPa, ultimate = 400 MPa).

Geometric constraints: height above operator SIP >= 800 mm; outside post-to-post 900 mm; post-to-seat clearance >= 50 mm. Mounted to chassis via 4x M12 bolts on a 500 x 80 mm pattern.

Load cases from ISO 3471 / OECD Code 4 scaled to 408 kg GVW: LC1 lateral 1.75 x GVW x g = 7000 N at top, max von Mises <= 295 MPa, DLV not penetrated. LC2 vertical crush 2.0 x GVW x g = 8000 N, max von Mises <= 267 MPa (400 / 1.5). LC3 longitudinal 0.5 x GVW x g = 2000 N, max von Mises <= 295 MPa. LC4 drawbar pull 1500 N with 80 kg operator weight; bolt-hole bearing <= 295 MPa. ROPS mass <= 12 kg.

prompt.title: Quarter-Scale Tractor ROPS welded assembly (ISO 3471 analog loads)
prompt.brief: Mild steel two-post rear-mount ROPS built up from 2 vertical roll-bar tubes + transverse cross tubes + 6-8 welded gusset plates + 2 chassis base plates + seat-back restraint bar. Sized to ISO 3471 analog loads on 408 kg GVW tractor; four static ISO cases; DLV not penetrated.

boundary_conditions.mounting: four M12 bolt holes on the two base plates fixed to chassis
boundary_conditions.loads: point loads at tube apex / drawbar point per ISO 3471

parts.name: main_roll_bar_vertical_tube
parts.role: vertical roll-bar post (left or right); primary bending member under LC1 lateral and LC2 vertical-crush; each post runs from the chassis base plate to the ROPS top
parts.count_note: Exactly 2 for a rear-mount two-post ROPS, per the original prompt.
parts.material_hint: ASTM A513 mild steel tube, cold-drawn or DOM

parts.name: transverse_cross_tube
parts.role: horizontal cross tube that ties the two vertical posts together at the top and optionally at mid-height; resists lateral load (LC1) by truss action and provides the ROPS top load-application point
parts.count_note: Minimum 2 (one at the top, one at mid-height or at the base for torsional stability); teams may use 3.

parts.name: welded_gusset_plate
parts.role: flat gusset plate welded at each tube-to-tube intersection and at each tube-to-baseplate weld to spread the weld stress and control the plastic hinge location during ISO 3471 loading
parts.count_note: Minimum 6 at the four top post-crosstube corners and two post-baseplate knees; ASABE entries typically use 6-8.

parts.name: chassis_mounting_base_plate
parts.role: thick steel base plate that the vertical post is welded to and the chassis bolts pass through; carries the full R1/R2/R3 moment into the chassis via the original sample's 500 x 80 mm M12 x 4 bolt pattern
parts.count_note: Exactly 2 (one per post). The original 4x M12 pattern is split as 2x M12 per plate.

geometric_constraints.bolt_spacing_mm: matches the 500 x 80 mm pattern (250 mm between plate centers x 80 mm across plate)

parts.name: seat_back_restraint_bar
parts.role: horizontal bar behind the operator seat that prevents rearward ejection during a rollover and bounds the Deflection Limiting Volume (DLV) check in R1b

geometric_constraints.orientation: horizontal, behind SIP at shoulder height

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: X = longitudinal (drawbar direction), Y = lateral, Z = vertical

pass_fail_criteria.description: Weld-zone DCR at every fillet weld in the frame (post-crosstube corners, post-baseplate knees, gusset-to-post edges, gusset-to-baseplate edges, seat-back-bar attachments) shall be <= 1.0 under the worst of LC1, LC2, LC3. Computed as peak weld-toe stress / weld-allowable stress.
4x M12 chassis-mounting bolt DCR shall be <= 1.0 under LC4 drawbar pull combined with 80 kg operator weight on the ROPS.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: second-order shell for tubes, second-order solid for gusset plates and base plates
analysis_types: linear static (x4 ISO 3471 cases) on assembled ROPS
optional non-linear plastic-hinge check under LC1 for DLV intrusion

interfaces.mediated_by: all-around fillet weld, reinforced by C-gusset on both sides
interfaces.load_path: lateral (LC1) and longitudinal (LC3) moment transfer from top of one post to the other via the cross tube

interfaces.mediated_by: all-around fillet weld at post foot, reinforced by C-gusset knee
interfaces.load_path: full ROPS top moment reacted into chassis base plate

interfaces.mediated_by: fillet welds on both gusset edges
interfaces.load_path: spreads post-crosstube and post-baseplate welds; controls plastic hinge location

interfaces.mediated_by: 4x M12 bolts on 500 x 80 mm pattern (2 per plate)
interfaces.load_path: ROPS base reaction into chassis; LC4 drawbar check loads this interface

interfaces.mediated_by: stub fillet weld on each post at shoulder height
interfaces.load_path: operator restraint load; also secondary lateral bracing

fem_check_policy.rationale: ISO 3471 evaluates ROPS as a welded frame: the DLV intrusion check (R1b) and the four ISO load cases are structurally coupled through the welded joints. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: The original prompt explicitly describes a welded tube frame (ASTM A513 tubing, weld map deliverable); ISO 3471 / SAE J2194 ROPS geometries are always welded assemblies of 12-18 weldments (two posts, cross tubes, gussets, base plates, seat-back bar). The FEM is inherently assembly-level because the weld geometry governs the plastic-hinge behavior.
notes.speculation_caveats: Gusset plan dimensions, cross-tube count, seat-back-bar diameter, and exact base-plate thickness are speculative; tube OD/wall, bolt pattern, and ISO 3471 scaled loads are anchored to the original sample.
